homepage.text_excerpt: Homepage | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97) Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas. Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97) Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas. Member of Parliament for North Cotswolds (The Cotswolds 1997-2024 and for Cirencester & Tewkesbury 1992-97) Farmer, Chartered Surveyor, and Chair of the Public Accounts Committee. Interests include the countryside, conservation, encouraging inward investment, and promoting British business overseas. In Pictures Visit to The Butterfly Garden 20 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited The Butterfly Garden, in Bamfurlong on Friday and met with Rapid Relief Team UK and members of Men in Sheds. Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visits Paxford Races 9 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited Paxford Races near Chipping Campden on Easter Monday to support the hunt and racing. Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown meets NFU members in Guiting Power 8 April 2026 Last week local MP Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown met with NFU members in Guiting Power to hear directly about the pressures facing local farm businesses. News Local Town and Parish Gloucester Fringe Infrastructure Group 2 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown attended the Local Town and Parish Gloucester Fringe Infrastructure Group in Quedgeley this morning. A417 Missing Link construction site visit 31 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited the A417 Missing Link construction site, where the new Project Director, Andrew Alcorn, and Assistant Project Manager, Alastair Clark gave him a drive‑through tour of the whole site. Meeting with Bibury Heritage 30 March 2026 This morning Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown met with Bibury Heritage to discuss plans to tackle congestion in the village. All News In Parliament Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown seeks reassurance on Royal Mail pension scheme transition after Capita contract termination 22 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown, Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, welcomes the Government statement on the administration of the Civil Service pension schemes and the decision to terminate Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme contract with Capita. He seeks reassurance from the Paymaster General on the future management of both schemes, and in particular on the transition arrangements for the Royal Mail scheme. Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for stronger, clearer powers to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping 20 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the Crime and Policing Bill, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs an amendment to create a specific law enabling police to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping and he cites the success of similar police powers in combatting hare-coursing. Clifton-Brown calls for priority heating oil support for very old and very young 16 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to ensure that the very old and the very young are given priority when allocating support to people reliant on heating oil. pages.title: About Sir Geoffrey | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown
headings: h3: Geoffrey was the Member of Parliament for Cirencester & Tewkesbury from 1992-1997, and has been the Member for the Cotswolds since 1997.
pages.text_excerpt: About Sir Geoffrey | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown F.R.I.C.S. Geoffrey was the Member of Parliament for Cirencester & Tewkesbury from 1992-1997, and has been the Member for the Cotswolds since 1997. Since becoming an MP in 1992 Geoffrey Clifton-Brown MP has served as the Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Rt. Hon Douglas Hogg QC MP, the Minister for Agriculture, Fisheries and Food; a Member of the Environment Select Committee and a Member of the Public Accounts House of Commons Select Committee. In October 2001, he was appointed a Shadow Frontbench Spokesman for Transport, Local Government & the Regions. In June 2002, he became a Shadow Minister for Local & Devolved Government Affairs. In March 2004, he was appointed as an Opposition Whip and in May 2005, he became Assistant Chief Whip. He is an active member of the Finance and Services Committee of the House since 2005. When David Cameron was elected as Leader of the Conservative Party, Geoffrey was appointed the Shadow Minister for Trade & Foreign Affairs. Geoffrey was later appointed in 2007 the Shadow Minister for International Development and Trade with responsibility for encouraging inward investment, promoting British business overseas and the World Trade Organisation, a role in which he served until the 2010 General Election. Between 2010 and 2015, Geoffrey was Chairman of the Conservative Party International Office, which saw him oversee all activity of the international wing of the party. This included the Westminster Foundation for Democracy, Conservative Friends of Groups, Conservatives Abroad, the Conservative Party’s Sister parties around the world and the Alliance of Conservative Reformists in Europe (ACRE) in Europe. He is the Vice-President of the ACRE. Sir Geoffrey is currently Chairman of the All Party Parliamentary Group on Shooting and Conservation, Treasurer of the 1922 Committee, Deputy Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, Chairman of the Conservative Friends of The Chinese and Member of the Finance Committee. He was knighted for Political and Public Service in 2018. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visits The Women's Centre at Gloucestershire Royal Hospital 19 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey is pictured with Craig and Olli who did a fantastic job of showing him around the maternity ward, birth unit and triage, and answered his many questions about the facilities and staffing. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown attends official opening of Brockworth Pharmacy and Health Clinic 2 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visited Brockworth Pharmacy and Health Clinic at Abbotswood Centre for its official grand opening, at the kind invitation of Pete Messiha of Ascent Wellfield, an independent pharmacy group. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown visits Ukraine 2 March 2026 Last week, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown attended the largest ever parliamentary delegation to Ukraine which took place on the four year anniversary of the full-scale invasion. pages.title: In Parliament | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown
pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey In Parliament Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown's speeches, debate interventions and questions to Ministers. In Parliament Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown seeks reassurance on Royal Mail pension scheme transition after Capita contract termination 22 April 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown, Chair of the Public Accounts Committee, welcomes the Government statement on the administration of the Civil Service pension schemes and the decision to terminate Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme contract with Capita. He seeks reassurance from the Paymaster General on the future management of both schemes, and in particular on the transition arrangements for the Royal Mail scheme. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for stronger, clearer powers to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping 20 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the Crime and Policing Bill, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs an amendment to create a specific law enabling police to seize vehicles used in fly-tipping and he cites the success of similar police powers in combatting hare-coursing. Image Clifton-Brown calls for priority heating oil support for very old and very young 16 March 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to ensure that the very old and the very young are given priority when allocating support to people reliant on heating oil. Image PAC Chair Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown urges Government to set date for Defence Investment Plan publication 4 March 2026 Speaking in today’s defence debate, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Government to set a publication date of the Defence Investment Plan so Parliament can scrutinise the plans to ensure the Government is able to reach its 3% target within 5 years. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for overhaul of SEND system in education funding debate 28 January 2026 Speaking in a debate on the distribution of education funding, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown highlights that Gloucestershire is among the lowest-funded areas for schools and raises concerns about SEND funding, calling on the Government to commit to a thorough overhaul of the SEND system. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown supports mandatory medical markers for firearms licence holders 28 January 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown backs calls for mandatory medical markers on GP records for firearms and shotgun certificate holders as essential for public safety and easy to operate. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for action to protect rural Britain 7 January 2026 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown condemns the Government for systematically disadvantaging rural communities which face higher living costs, funding cuts, rising business rates, and the persistent threat from the family farm tax despite compromises. He warns against policies endangering vital rural sectors such as farming, hospitality, trail hunting, and shooting. These sectors contribute billions to the economy and underpin the rural way of life, and failing to act risks destroying livelihoods and the very fabric of the countryside. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls for democracy and fair use of Venezuela’s oil wealth 5 January 2026 Following the Government’s statement on the situation in Venezuela, Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown calls on the Foreign Secretary to make the case for the rules-based international system at the United Nations, and to engage with the United States Administration on a realistic plan to restore democracy in Venezuela and ensure that the country’s oil wealth benefits the Venezuelan people. Image Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown urges Government not to change shooting or trail hunting laws 5 January 2026 Si

pages.title: Campaigns and Policy | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown
pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns and Policy | Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Sir Geoffrey News In Parliament Public Accounts Committee Campaigns and Policy Gallery Contact Search Business Survey Campaigns and Policy Campaign groups often organise e-mail write-in campaigns to MPs. Here are some of Sir Geoffrey’s responses to these campaigns. Campaigns and Policy Consultation on Proposals to Prohibit Trail Hunting in England and Wales 28 April 2026 I have been contacted by many hundreds of constituents on the application of the Hunting Act 2004 and the Government’s proposals to impose a ban on trail hunting, which is lawful within that Act. WASPI Women 25 February 2026 After much delay, the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions gave a statement on 29th January 2026 reaffirming their decision to not compensate those impacted. Social Media ban for under-16s 26 January 2026 I have received many hundreds of emails regarding banning social media for under-16s. Planning and Infrastructure Bill - House of Lord's Amendments 19 November 2025 When these amendments returned to the House of Commons on the 13th of November, in the form of Lords amendments 38 and 40, the Government put down motions to disagree, meaning they did not support the amendments. Gaza 29 July 2025 My approach on this matter has been consistent. You may be interested to know that on the 10th June I raised this in the House of Commons. 2024 2022 2020 2018 2023 2021 2019 Sir Geoffrey Clifton-Brown MP House of Commons London, SW1A 0AA Tel: 020 7219 5147 Email: CLIFTONBROWNG@parliament.uk Contact Cotswold District: Blockley; Bourton Vale; Bourton Village; Campden & Vale; Chedworth & Churn Valley; Coln Valley; Ermin; Fosseridge; Moreton East; Moreton West; Northleach; Sandywell; Stow; The Rissingtons.
